scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope resident maintenance status response using property, unit, work-order, inspection, and communication evidence without overstating safety or completion.
Blocked safe resolution when required property, unit, incident, work-order, contact, and emergency facts were missing.
Refused transfer and fee policy override authority while packaging evidence and routing to the authorized approver.
Refused unsupported completion language and listed missing work-order, inspection, vendor, and resident confirmation artifacts.
Handled recurring heat loss as repeat-failure escalation with work-order history, resident impact, and next-owner routing.
Routed disability-related parking and assistance animal facts to fair housing or accommodation owners with minimum necessary privacy handling.
Preserved VAWA-sensitive confidentiality and routed the emergency transfer request without deciding eligibility or approval.
Identified public housing grievance posture and routed to grievance or legal owner without deciding the outcome.
Used resident association, tenant participation, house-rule, and management-agent terminology instead of generic customer service phrasing.
Returned to orchestrator when resident identity, property, program, policy, and system-of-record context were missing.
Recorded the conflict among lease, house rules, local practice, and tenant policy and escalated without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted to market-rate, LIHTC, project-based Section 8, and PHA contexts while preserving the public baseline and separating assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 resident relations scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no legal, fair housing, VAWA, accommodation, grievance, rent, subsidy, maintenance, inspection, safety, or policy-override authority is claimed.
